threadTitle: Things that instantly reveal you just moved here
body: You still check your watch for Tube times instead of just winging it like the rest of us. That’s how you know a newbie.
Last night on the 59, a guy serenaded the whole bus with 80s love songs. Everyone went from grumpy to grinning, it was like a spontaneous gig.

threadTitle: When you knew you were actually living here
body: When the street vendor remembered my order at the kebab stand and joked about my favorite sauce - that's when I felt at home.

threadTitle: Things Berlin does that nowhere else does
body: Only in Friedrichshain will you find a bar serving cocktails alongside vintage arcade games. It’s like a nostalgic time capsule – neon lights and all.

threadTitle: Real signs someone is from here vs just working here
body: You know someone’s a local when they don’t even flinch at a street vendor yelling about the latest BART chaos. It’s just another Tuesday.

threadTitle: Muni moments that define this city
body: Caught a glimpse of a guy walking his pet pig down Larkin while we waited for the delayed 19. Only in SF, right? Classic Muni moment.

threadTitle: Things tourists get wrong about living here
body: Tourists think Gràcia is all about Gaudí, but the real charm is in the hidden plazas where locals sip vermut, not in the tourist traps.

threadTitle: How to spot a recent arrival
body: You can tell a recent arrival by their enthusiasm for paella at every meal—locals know it’s only for Sundays here.

threadTitle: Things that instantly reveal you just moved here
body: You order a pint and say 'please,' instead of just 'lager.' The bartenders look at you like you just popped out of a time machine.
Caught the 12 last night, a guy was singing old school garage tracks. Whole bus joined in, felt like an impromptu rave. Only in Peckham!

threadTitle: When you knew you were actually living here
body: I knew I was home when I saw a kid selling homemade lemonade on a corner, next to a couple arguing over last night’s football match. Pure Neukölln.

threadTitle: Things Berlin does that nowhere else does
body: Only in Kreuzberg can you have a kebab at 3 AM, then go find a pop-up techno party under a bridge. It's a weird magic that just works here.

threadTitle: Real signs someone is from here vs just working here
body: You know someone’s from here when they can name the BART delays like old friends—"Oh, the 22’s just always late, but you’ll see the 19 right on time."

threadTitle: Muni moments that define this city
body: Saw a guy serenading commuters at the Civic Center stop after the BART evacuation. Only in SF would a crisis turn into an impromptu concert.

threadTitle: Things tourists get wrong about living here
body: Tourists think Gràcia is just about Gaudí, but the real gems are the tiny plazas filled with more locals than tourists, especially after dark.

threadTitle: How to spot a recent arrival
body: You can tell a recent arrival by their bewilderment at the 7-minute wait for a tram. Locals know it’s just part of life here.
